---
title: "Stop dist-upgrade / partial upgrade"
date: 2013-03-01
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by reedk on 2013-03-01
So, without any conscious act on my part, my 12.04 system is prompting me for a partial upgrade on every run of Update Manager, which makes me thing it's trying to do a dist-upgrade. It's not what I want - I want to stay on 12.04. Is there a way to stop it from trying to do this upgrade?

If I run
```
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade
```
I get 
```
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.
```


Screenshot of the error message:
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

---

### Post by Old_Grey_Wolf on 2013-03-01
Something is wrong with the screenshot you attached. I can't view it.

dist-upgrade will not upgrade you to 12.10. To upgrade to 12.10 you would have to do a do-release-upgrade. 

Do you get any error messages from this command?
> **reedk said:**
> 
If I run
```
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade
```


---

### Post by reedk on 2013-03-01
No, no error at all. That's what's odd. Just the pop up window when Update Manager runs. The error is the standard "Partial Upgrade" message that can come up, with options to perform a partial upgrade or cancel. I did not know that about do-release-upgrade - thanks for the info.

---

### Post by Old_Grey_Wolf on 2013-03-01
> **reedk said:**
> No, no error at all. That's what's odd. Just the pop up window when Update Manager runs. The error is the standard "Partial Upgrade" message that can come up, with options to perform a partial upgrade or cancel. I did not know that about do-release-upgrade - thanks for the info.

I wish I could view the screenshot. No error messages. Well, if you have a backup of important files then do ```
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get dist-upgrade
```

Post any errors you get.

---

### Post by grahammechanical on 2013-03-01
For the record do not accept that offer. Just click continue and run the update as usual. Those of us who run the next release of Ubuntu while it is being developed get that message from time to time. I have been getting it for the last few days on 13.04. My guess is that Update Manager is confused. Some libraries are being updated and they depend on other libraries that are not yet in the repositories. Therefore those libraries are not being brought in. So, Update Manager is offering a Partial Upgrade as if you are partway through an upgrade.

If you accept that offer then older libraries will be removed without their replacements being available and your system will break. I had it happen to me. Just ignore the message and over the next few days the situation will right itself. How often do you update? You might try doing it daily for a while.

Regards.

---

### Post by Old_Grey_Wolf on 2013-03-01
If you indeed do have a partial update then enter these commands:
```
sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get dist-upgrade

```

---

### Post by reedk on 2013-03-01
You may be onto something. After being away for a few hours, I sat down to look into this further and no longer get the error message from Update Manager. My update schedule is weekly (for non-security upgrades). I'm suprised to see that on an LTS (I don't recall it ever happening betwixt 10.04 and 12.04) but the behavior matches your description. I'll ignore it for another week and if I still have issues I'll try the dist-upgrade recommended by Old_Grey_Wolf. Then I'll come back and update it as solved or investigate further.

Thanks all!

---

### Post by jcoles on 2013-03-20
Xubuntu 12.10 here. I took the partial upgrade. Advice: AVOID!!!
Wine was removed, but not upgraded. I hope nothing else was lost. 
I hope it helps that I opted to keep the package files rather than remove them, which the upgrader said could take hours. Some of those files looked important. 

I'm starting to wonder if this is even legit. I take every regular update that's offered. So how could I need some special "upgrade" this time. 

I was able to reinstall wine. 

Can anyone explain what the partial upgrade was supposed to achieve? 
Experienced hands: is it best to ignore the "partial upgrade" notice, and just update as normal?

---

### Post by reedk on 2013-03-26
I follwed the advice of[ 						 					]("http://ubuntuforums.org/member.php?u=1087323") 				 				 					 						 	[**[COLOR=#000000]grahammechanical[/COLOR]**]("http://ubuntuforums.org/member.php?u=1087323") and ignored the partial upgrade option, upgrading normally. After a few days, it stopped prompting me.

---
